2603.29405.title: Hallucination-aware intermediate representation edit in large vision-language models
2603.29405.paper_summary: Large Vision-Language Models have demonstrated exceptional performance in multimodal reasoning and complex scene understanding. However, these models still face significant hallucination issues, where outputs contradict visual facts. Recent research on hallucination mitigation has focused on retraining methods and Contrastive Decoding (CD) methods. While both methods perform well, retraining methods require substantial training resources, and CD methods introduce dual inference overhead. These factors hinder their practical applicability. To address the above issue, we propose a framework for dynamically detecting hallucination representations and performing hallucination-eliminating edits on these representations. With minimal additional computational cost, we achieve state-of-the-art performance on existing benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, highlighting its efficient and robust hallucination elimination capability and its powerful controllability over hallucinations. Code is available at https://github.com/ASGO-MM/HIRE
2603.29405.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺-語言模型在多模態推理和複雜場景理解方面表現出色。<lb/>然而，這些模型仍然面臨著顯著的幻覺問題，即輸出與視覺事實相矛盾。<lb/>最近對幻覺緩解的研究集中在重新訓練方法和對比解碼（CD）方法上。<lb/>雖然這兩種方法表現良好，但重新訓練方法需要大量的訓練資源，而CD方法則引入了雙重推理的開銷。<lb/>這些因素阻礙了它們的實際應用。<lb/>為了解決上述問題，我們提出了一個框架，用於動態檢測幻覺表示並對這些表示進行消除幻覺的編輯。<lb/>在最小的額外計算成本下，我們在現有基準上達到了最先進的性能。<lb/>大量實驗證明了我們方法的有效性，突顯了其高效且穩健的幻覺消除能力以及對幻覺的強大可控性。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/ASGO-MM/HIRE 獲得。
2603.29405.author: Wei Suo et.al.
2603.29405.authors: Wei Suo, Hanzu Zhang, Lijun Zhang, Ji Ma, Peng Wang, Yanning Zhang
